summary: Lock-everything-except-the-tease monetization update — backend regression + new weekly_promo plan validated. 30/30 pytest tests pass (100%) against the public preview URL. New tests added: (1) POST /api/checkout/session {plan:'weekly_promo'} for a registered user returns 200 or 502 with placeholder STRIPE_API_KEY (accepted — code-level config is correct: PRICES['weekly_promo'] = {amount:499, mode:'subscription', interval:'week', trial_days:3}); (2) guest user blocked from weekly_promo with 400 'create an account'. All previous behavior unchanged: weekly $6.99, yearly $39.99, lifetime $59.99 (mode=payment), monthly returns 400, invalid plan returns 400, guest blocked from checkout, register→quiz→generate (200)→generate (402 'Free preview used') E2E flow intact, library/categories/daily endpoints OK, no _id leaks.

minor.issue: Returns 502 'Invalid API Key provided: sk_test_****gent' — expected per review_request (placeholder key). To fully verify recurring.interval='week' and subscription_data.trial_period_days=3 reach Stripe for weekly_promo, replace STRIPE_API_KEY with a real Stripe test secret key. Code inspection of /app/backend/server.py PRICES + checkout_session() confirms payload is built identically to 'weekly' with amount=499 instead of 699.

weekly_promo has no end-date or auto-expiry logic in backend; it's a perpetual SKU. If product wants the promo to be limited-window or one-time-per-user, that needs to be enforced (currently any user can call POST /api/checkout/session {plan:'weekly_promo'} unlimited times).

action_items: Replace STRIPE_API_KEY placeholder 'sk_test_emergent' in /app/backend/.env with a real Stripe test secret key to E2E-validate that weekly_promo correctly creates a $4.99/week subscription with trial_period_days=3 on Stripe.

Decide whether weekly_promo should be gated (e.g., only offered once per user, or only inside the discount-paywall variant) — currently no server-side enforcement exists; any authenticated non-guest user can call it directly.
critical_code_review_comments: weekly_promo entry in PRICES is a clean copy of weekly with amount=499 and the same interval/trial_days — reuses existing checkout_session() branching without code duplication. Good.

Frontend lock pattern (first-3 lines per category + locked favorites + paywall-on-Generate-when-quota-zero) is purely client-side as noted; backend has no new endpoints/state to validate beyond the existing 402 path on /api/icebreakers/generate, which still works correctly.
server.py is now ~720 lines — still exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers/{auth,icebreakers,checkout}.py.
checkout_session still maps all Stripe errors (including auth failures with the placeholder key) to 502. Consider 500 for config-level errors so monitoring can distinguish ours-vs-Stripe.
PRICES has no programmatic guard against amount==0 or trial_days<0 — minor, currently all values are hard-coded constants.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_icebreaker_api.py (added 2 tests for weekly_promo: test_weekly_promo_plan_creates_session_or_502, test_weekly_promo_guest_blocked; updated TestPricingConfigShape to assert '"weekly_promo"' and '499' present in PRICES; renamed test_prices_dict_has_only_3_plans -> test_prices_dict_has_expected_plans)
seed_data_creation: Tests create ephemeral users with email pattern test_<uuid>@example.com and guest sessions. No persistent seed data created. Library seeds auto-loaded on backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: cd /app && pytest backend/tests/test_icebreaker_api.py -v --tb=short. ~35s end-to-end (one real Claude call). All 4 Stripe checkout tests (weekly, weekly_promo, yearly, lifetime) accept 200 OR 502 because STRIPE_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env is placeholder 'sk_test_emergent'. PRICES currently has 4 plans: weekly($6.99), weekly_promo($4.99), yearly($39.99), lifetime($59.99). Frontend monetization lock changes (first-3-lines, locked favorites, no-API paywall-on-Generate) are purely client-side and were intentionally not in scope.
rca_of_issue: Not applicable — no regressions found. weekly_promo plan was added to PRICES correctly and behaves identically to weekly with a $2 lower amount; the checkout_session() handler picks it up via the same code path.
